analysis: Seven nights in a row above six hours, with four of them clearing seven and a half — that's not a fluke, that's a body that's being genuinely looked after. Your best night was the 8th, nearly eight hours with over two hours of REM and only half an hour awake, which is the kind of sleep that makes you look annoyingly good the next day. The awake time has been the wobbliest part — that 1h 17m on the 10th and 1h 8m on the 7th are worth watching, because you're clearly getting into bed and doing the right things, the time you're losing is in the middle of the night rather than at the start. Deep sleep is sitting a little low across the whole stretch, mostly in that 26–53 minute range, so whatever you're doing to wind down is landing for REM but your body might want a slightly earlier finish to really cash in on the deep stuff.
